Records.authors: Henn, Johannes, Pratt, Elizabeth, Sattelberger, Anna-Laura, Zoia, Simone
Records.description: Four-loop planar ladder integral with one off-shell external leg ($p_4^2 \neq 0$, $p_1^2=p_2^2=p_3^2=0$), 13 massless propagators, and a numerator factor $(p_2+p_3)^2$ together with the normalization $((p_1+p_2)^2)^{2(6-d)}$. The integral depends on two dimensionless ratios $y_2 = (s-p_4^2)/p_4^2$ and $y_3 = (p_4^2-s-t)/p_4^2$. The paper studies the maximal cut in $d=4$. The $D_2$-ideal generated by Picard-Fuchs operators is holonomic of rank 4; solutions are homogeneous of degree 0 in $y_2,y_3$, reducing the system to a single ODE in $y=y_2/y_3$. The four canonical series solutions are: $1$, $\log(y)$, $\log(y)^2+2\operatorname{Li}_2(-y)$, and $\log(y)^3-12\operatorname{Li}_3(-y)+6\log(y)\operatorname{Li}_2(-y)$. Singular locus: $\{y_2 \, y_3 \, (y_2+y_3)=0\}$. Tools: LiteRed for IBP identities, FiniteFlow for solving, HolonomicFunctions for Groebner basis in the rational Weyl algebra, Singular for Weyl closure computation.
Records.location: Appendix C (Section C), Eq. (C.2), Fig. 6; Picard-Fuchs operators Eq. (C.5); Groebner basis Eq. (C.6); series solutions Eqs. (C.17)-(C.18)
